title: /bat
text: /bat Start the application in batch mode. /bat { param_1 param_2 ...} Use With ide.exe (Uniface IDE: /bat /tst only) User applications Description Start the application in batch mode. An application running in batch mode has the following characteristics: User input is not possible. You should avoid executing ProcScript statements that require user input, such as askmess and print/ask . Output to the screen is not allowed. You should avoid executing ProcScript statements that request screen output, such as edit , display , and message . The message frame is disabled. Messages normally sent to the message frame (with the ProcScript statement putmess , for example) are redirected to the standard system output device. This is usually the terminal screen. The /tfo and /tfi switches are not allowed. Optionally, you can provide one or more parameters to the batch application via general variables ($1-$99). The first parameter is available in $1 , the second in $2 , and so on. The parameters do not need to directly follow the /bat switch. You can use the ProcScript function $batch to test if an application is running in batch or interactively. Related Links $batch Return the batch mode indicator. Passing Parameters to an Application The parameters to be passed to your application must be the last parameters entered on the command line. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
